{{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '群馬県渋川市祖母島499', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '群馬県吾妻郡東吾妻町箱島', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takeuchi-jinja}} [[JA:武内神社 (渋川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q66085129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takeuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Kawasukune-jinja approach.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}} Obashima 499 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), disputed shrine
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}) September 21 |Chief priest = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Sukune Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}}
{{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Takeuchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeuchi Shrine''' (たけうちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Obashima, {{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}}. The enshrined deity is Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma District|Q5617131|WD=Q5617131|1=Gunma District, Gunma}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===History=== It is said that the shrine originated from being divided from {{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Shibukawa City Kawashima)|Q66085130|WD=Q66085130|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Shibukawa)}} in Kawashima on September 21 of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}){{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. As indicated by a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} (1708) that records the name “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin,” the shrine was known as “Sukune Shrine” until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}}), it was renamed “Takeuchi Shrine”{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The {{ill|lt=tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} on the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears the inscription “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin.” {{ill|lt=Kizao Ozaki|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} proposed that Takeuchi Shrine (Sukune Shrine) was originally Kawanami no Sukune Shrine. He interpreted “Kawanami” as “river” and “Sukune” as “chokune,” understanding the shrine name to mean “the main current of the river.” He explained that because three shrines—{{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma Town)|Q66085131|WD=Q66085131|lt=Hakoshima|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma)}}, Obashima, and Kawashima—were lined up along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}} in places with “shima” (island) in their names, they represented the deification of the Agatsuma River itself.{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Studies on the Jinmyōchō of Kōzuke Province |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |pages=210-212 |last=Ozaki |author=Kizao Ozaki |first=Kizao |author-link=Kizao Ozaki |doi=10.11501/12266236}}{{Registration required}} An engraved inscription dated the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|lt=1711|Q6866|WD=Q6866|1=1711}}) is found on the {{ill|lt=gibōshi|Q3105399|WD=Q3105399|1=Giboshi}} of the balustrade of the main hall, and thus that year is considered the year of construction{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The shrine buildings are located atop a high ground known as Ubazuka{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. Although it was once thought to be a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, it is in fact a natural {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|p=117}}. There is a local tradition that this was the place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}’s wife {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwagitsuhime|1=UNKNOWN}} and their child lived, and that Ubazuka is the grave of Uwagitsuhime, which is said to be the origin of the place name Obashima{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1984|p=903}}. ===Grounds=== Kawasukune-jinja first torii.jpg|First torii Kawasukune-jinja first torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the first torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii.jpg|Second torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the second torii Kawasukune-jinja Worship Hall.jpg|Shrine buildings ===Festivals=== * April 3 – Spring annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * July 25 – Gion Festival; a festival of the enshrined Yasaka Shrine, during which a portable shrine is paraded{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}}. * October 9 – Autumn annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===Access=== * 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obashima Station|Q7876424|WD=Q7876424|1=Ubashima Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma Line|Q391568|WD=Q391568|1=Agatsuma Line}} of {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === {{Commonscat}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Gazetteer |date=1925-10-20 |year=1925 |publisher=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |ref=harv |author=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |doi=10.11501/1020918}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1993-3-31 |year=1993 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 2 General History, Part 1: Prehistory to Early Modern |doi=10.11501/9644681}}{{Registration required}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 4 Folklore |doi=10.11501/9643284}}{{Registration required}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:渋川市の建築物]] [[Category:渋川市の歴史]] [[Category:武内宿禰]]

Commons category (P373) == * Takeuchi-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Kahasukuneno Shrine|lt=Kahasukuneno Shrine|WD=Q135039964|1=Kahasukuneno Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '群馬県渋川市祖母島499', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '群馬県吾妻郡東吾妻町箱島', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takeuchi-jinja}} [[JA:武内神社 (渋川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q66085129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takeuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Kawasukune-jinja approach.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}} Obashima 499 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), disputed shrine
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}) September 21 |Chief priest = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Sukune Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}}
{{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Takeuchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeuchi Shrine''' (たけうちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Obashima, {{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}}. The enshrined deity is Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma District|Q5617131|WD=Q5617131|1=Gunma District, Gunma}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===History=== It is said that the shrine originated from being divided from {{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Shibukawa City Kawashima)|Q66085130|WD=Q66085130|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Shibukawa)}} in Kawashima on September 21 of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}){{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. As indicated by a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} (1708) that records the name “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin,” the shrine was known as “Sukune Shrine” until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}}), it was renamed “Takeuchi Shrine”{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The {{ill|lt=tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} on the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears the inscription “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin.” {{ill|lt=Kizao Ozaki|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} proposed that Takeuchi Shrine (Sukune Shrine) was originally Kawanami no Sukune Shrine. He interpreted “Kawanami” as “river” and “Sukune” as “chokune,” understanding the shrine name to mean “the main current of the river.” He explained that because three shrines—{{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma Town)|Q66085131|WD=Q66085131|lt=Hakoshima|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma)}}, Obashima, and Kawashima—were lined up along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}} in places with “shima” (island) in their names, they represented the deification of the Agatsuma River itself.{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Studies on the Jinmyōchō of Kōzuke Province |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |pages=210-212 |last=Ozaki |author=Kizao Ozaki |first=Kizao |author-link=Kizao Ozaki |doi=10.11501/12266236}}{{Registration required}} An engraved inscription dated the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|lt=1711|Q6866|WD=Q6866|1=1711}}) is found on the {{ill|lt=gibōshi|Q3105399|WD=Q3105399|1=Giboshi}} of the balustrade of the main hall, and thus that year is considered the year of construction{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The shrine buildings are located atop a high ground known as Ubazuka{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. Although it was once thought to be a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, it is in fact a natural {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|p=117}}. There is a local tradition that this was the place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}’s wife {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwagitsuhime|1=UNKNOWN}} and their child lived, and that Ubazuka is the grave of Uwagitsuhime, which is said to be the origin of the place name Obashima{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1984|p=903}}. ===Grounds=== Kawasukune-jinja first torii.jpg|First torii Kawasukune-jinja first torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the first torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii.jpg|Second torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the second torii Kawasukune-jinja Worship Hall.jpg|Shrine buildings ===Festivals=== * April 3 – Spring annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * July 25 – Gion Festival; a festival of the enshrined Yasaka Shrine, during which a portable shrine is paraded{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}}. * October 9 – Autumn annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===Access=== * 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obashima Station|Q7876424|WD=Q7876424|1=Ubashima Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma Line|Q391568|WD=Q391568|1=Agatsuma Line}} of {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === {{Commonscat}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Gazetteer |date=1925-10-20 |year=1925 |publisher=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |ref=harv |author=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |doi=10.11501/1020918}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1993-3-31 |year=1993 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 2 General History, Part 1: Prehistory to Early Modern |doi=10.11501/9644681}}{{Registration required}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 4 Folklore |doi=10.11501/9643284}}{{Registration required}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:渋川市の建築物]] [[Category:渋川市の歴史]] [[Category:武内宿禰]]

{{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '群馬県渋川市祖母島499', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '群馬県吾妻郡東吾妻町箱島', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takeuchi-jinja}} [[JA:武内神社 (渋川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q66085129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takeuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Kawasukune-jinja approach.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}} Obashima 499 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), disputed shrine
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}) September 21 |Chief priest = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Sukune Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}}
{{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Takeuchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeuchi Shrine''' (たけうちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Obashima, {{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}}. The enshrined deity is Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma District|Q5617131|WD=Q5617131|1=Gunma District, Gunma}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===History=== It is said that the shrine originated from being divided from {{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Shibukawa City Kawashima)|Q66085130|WD=Q66085130|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Shibukawa)}} in Kawashima on September 21 of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}){{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. As indicated by a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} (1708) that records the name “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin,” the shrine was known as “Sukune Shrine” until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}}), it was renamed “Takeuchi Shrine”{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The {{ill|lt=tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} on the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears the inscription “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin.” {{ill|lt=Kizao Ozaki|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} proposed that Takeuchi Shrine (Sukune Shrine) was originally Kawanami no Sukune Shrine. He interpreted “Kawanami” as “river” and “Sukune” as “chokune,” understanding the shrine name to mean “the main current of the river.” He explained that because three shrines—{{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma Town)|Q66085131|WD=Q66085131|lt=Hakoshima|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma)}}, Obashima, and Kawashima—were lined up along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}} in places with “shima” (island) in their names, they represented the deification of the Agatsuma River itself.{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Studies on the Jinmyōchō of Kōzuke Province |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |pages=210-212 |last=Ozaki |author=Kizao Ozaki |first=Kizao |author-link=Kizao Ozaki |doi=10.11501/12266236}}{{Registration required}} An engraved inscription dated the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|lt=1711|Q6866|WD=Q6866|1=1711}}) is found on the {{ill|lt=gibōshi|Q3105399|WD=Q3105399|1=Giboshi}} of the balustrade of the main hall, and thus that year is considered the year of construction{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The shrine buildings are located atop a high ground known as Ubazuka{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. Although it was once thought to be a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, it is in fact a natural {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|p=117}}. There is a local tradition that this was the place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}’s wife {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwagitsuhime|1=UNKNOWN}} and their child lived, and that Ubazuka is the grave of Uwagitsuhime, which is said to be the origin of the place name Obashima{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1984|p=903}}. ===Grounds=== Kawasukune-jinja first torii.jpg|First torii Kawasukune-jinja first torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the first torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii.jpg|Second torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the second torii Kawasukune-jinja Worship Hall.jpg|Shrine buildings ===Festivals=== * April 3 – Spring annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * July 25 – Gion Festival; a festival of the enshrined Yasaka Shrine, during which a portable shrine is paraded{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}}. * October 9 – Autumn annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===Access=== * 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obashima Station|Q7876424|WD=Q7876424|1=Ubashima Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma Line|Q391568|WD=Q391568|1=Agatsuma Line}} of {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === {{Commonscat}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Gazetteer |date=1925-10-20 |year=1925 |publisher=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |ref=harv |author=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |doi=10.11501/1020918}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1993-3-31 |year=1993 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 2 General History, Part 1: Prehistory to Early Modern |doi=10.11501/9644681}}{{Registration required}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 4 Folklore |doi=10.11501/9643284}}{{Registration required}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:渋川市の建築物]] [[Category:渋川市の歴史]] [[Category:Takeshiuchi no Sukune]]

category|Category:Takeuchi-jinja}} [[JA:武内神社 (渋川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q66085129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takeuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Kawasukune-jinja approach.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}} Obashima 499 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), disputed shrine
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}) September 21 |Chief priest = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Sukune Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}}
{{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Takeuchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeuchi Shrine''' (たけうちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Obashima, {{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}}. The enshrined deity is Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma District|Q5617131|WD=Q5617131|1=Gunma District, Gunma}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===History=== It is said that the shrine originated from being divided from {{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Shibukawa City Kawashima)|Q66085130|WD=Q66085130|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Shibukawa)}} in Kawashima on September 21 of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}){{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. As indicated by a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} (1708) that records the name “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin,” the shrine was known as “Sukune Shrine” until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}}), it was renamed “Takeuchi Shrine”{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The {{ill|lt=tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} on the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears the inscription “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin.” {{ill|lt=Kizao Ozaki|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} proposed that Takeuchi Shrine (Sukune Shrine) was originally Kawanami no Sukune Shrine. He interpreted “Kawanami” as “river” and “Sukune” as “chokune,” understanding the shrine name to mean “the main current of the river.” He explained that because three shrines—{{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma Town)|Q66085131|WD=Q66085131|lt=Hakoshima|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma)}}, Obashima, and Kawashima—were lined up along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}} in places with “shima” (island) in their names, they represented the deification of the Agatsuma River itself.{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Studies on the Jinmyōchō of Kōzuke Province |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |pages=210-212 |last=Ozaki |author=Kizao Ozaki |first=Kizao |author-link=Kizao Ozaki |doi=10.11501/12266236}}{{Registration required}} An engraved inscription dated the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|lt=1711|Q6866|WD=Q6866|1=1711}}) is found on the {{ill|lt=gibōshi|Q3105399|WD=Q3105399|1=Giboshi}} of the balustrade of the main hall, and thus that year is considered the year of construction{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The shrine buildings are located atop a high ground known as Ubazuka{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. Although it was once thought to be a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, it is in fact a natural {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|p=117}}. There is a local tradition that this was the place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}’s wife {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwagitsuhime|1=UNKNOWN}} and their child lived, and that Ubazuka is the grave of Uwagitsuhime, which is said to be the origin of the place name Obashima{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1984|p=903}}. ===Grounds=== Kawasukune-jinja first torii.jpg|First torii Kawasukune-jinja first torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the first torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii.jpg|Second torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the second torii Kawasukune-jinja Worship Hall.jpg|Shrine buildings ===Festivals=== * April 3 – Spring annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * July 25 – Gion Festival; a festival of the enshrined Yasaka Shrine, during which a portable shrine is paraded{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}}. * October 9 – Autumn annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===Access=== * 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obashima Station|Q7876424|WD=Q7876424|1=Ubashima Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma Line|Q391568|WD=Q391568|1=Agatsuma Line}} of {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === {{Commonscat}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Gazetteer |date=1925-10-20 |year=1925 |publisher=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |ref=harv |author=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |doi=10.11501/1020918}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1993-3-31 |year=1993 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 2 General History, Part 1: Prehistory to Early Modern |doi=10.11501/9644681}}{{Registration required}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 4 Folklore |doi=10.11501/9643284}}{{Registration required}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧村社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:渋川市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Shibukawa, Gunma]] [[Category:Takeshiuchi no Sukune]]

category|Category:Takeuchi-jinja}} [[JA:武内神社 (渋川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q66085129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takeuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Kawasukune-jinja approach.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}} Obashima 499 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), disputed shrine
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}) September 21 |Chief priest = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Sukune Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}}
{{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Takeuchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeuchi Shrine''' (たけうちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Obashima, {{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}}. The enshrined deity is Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma District|Q5617131|WD=Q5617131|1=Gunma District, Gunma}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===History=== It is said that the shrine originated from being divided from {{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Shibukawa City Kawashima)|Q66085130|WD=Q66085130|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Shibukawa)}} in Kawashima on September 21 of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}){{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. As indicated by a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} (1708) that records the name “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin,” the shrine was known as “Sukune Shrine” until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}}), it was renamed “Takeuchi Shrine”{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The {{ill|lt=tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} on the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears the inscription “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin.” {{ill|lt=Kizao Ozaki|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} proposed that Takeuchi Shrine (Sukune Shrine) was originally Kawanami no Sukune Shrine. He interpreted “Kawanami” as “river” and “Sukune” as “chokune,” understanding the shrine name to mean “the main current of the river.” He explained that because three shrines—{{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma Town)|Q66085131|WD=Q66085131|lt=Hakoshima|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma)}}, Obashima, and Kawashima—were lined up along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}} in places with “shima” (island) in their names, they represented the deification of the Agatsuma River itself.{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Studies on the Jinmyōchō of Kōzuke Province |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |pages=210-212 |last=Ozaki |author=Kizao Ozaki |first=Kizao |author-link=Kizao Ozaki |doi=10.11501/12266236}}{{Registration required}} An engraved inscription dated the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|lt=1711|Q6866|WD=Q6866|1=1711}}) is found on the {{ill|lt=gibōshi|Q3105399|WD=Q3105399|1=Giboshi}} of the balustrade of the main hall, and thus that year is considered the year of construction{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The shrine buildings are located atop a high ground known as Ubazuka{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. Although it was once thought to be a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, it is in fact a natural {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|p=117}}. There is a local tradition that this was the place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}’s wife {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwagitsuhime|1=UNKNOWN}} and their child lived, and that Ubazuka is the grave of Uwagitsuhime, which is said to be the origin of the place name Obashima{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1984|p=903}}. ===Grounds=== Kawasukune-jinja first torii.jpg|First torii Kawasukune-jinja first torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the first torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii.jpg|Second torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the second torii Kawasukune-jinja Worship Hall.jpg|Shrine buildings ===Festivals=== * April 3 – Spring annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * July 25 – Gion Festival; a festival of the enshrined Yasaka Shrine, during which a portable shrine is paraded{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}}. * October 9 – Autumn annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===Access=== * 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obashima Station|Q7876424|WD=Q7876424|1=Ubashima Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma Line|Q391568|WD=Q391568|1=Agatsuma Line}} of {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === {{Commonscat}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Gazetteer |date=1925-10-20 |year=1925 |publisher=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |ref=harv |author=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |doi=10.11501/1020918}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1993-3-31 |year=1993 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 2 General History, Part 1: Prehistory to Early Modern |doi=10.11501/9644681}}{{Registration required}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 4 Folklore |doi=10.11501/9643284}}{{Registration required}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:渋川市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Shibukawa, Gunma]] [[Category:Takeshiuchi no Sukune]]

category|Category:Takeuchi-jinja}} [[JA:武内神社 (渋川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q66085129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takeuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Kawasukune-jinja approach.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}} Obashima 499 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), disputed shrine
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}) September 21 |Chief priest = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Sukune Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}}
{{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Takeuchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeuchi Shrine''' (たけうちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Obashima, {{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}}. The enshrined deity is Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma District|Q5617131|WD=Q5617131|1=Gunma District, Gunma}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===History=== It is said that the shrine originated from being divided from {{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Shibukawa City Kawashima)|Q66085130|WD=Q66085130|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Shibukawa)}} in Kawashima on September 21 of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}){{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. As indicated by a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} (1708) that records the name “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin,” the shrine was known as “Sukune Shrine” until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}}), it was renamed “Takeuchi Shrine”{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The {{ill|lt=tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} on the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears the inscription “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin.” {{ill|lt=Kizao Ozaki|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} proposed that Takeuchi Shrine (Sukune Shrine) was originally Kawanami no Sukune Shrine. He interpreted “Kawanami” as “river” and “Sukune” as “chokune,” understanding the shrine name to mean “the main current of the river.” He explained that because three shrines—{{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma Town)|Q66085131|WD=Q66085131|lt=Hakoshima|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma)}}, Obashima, and Kawashima—were lined up along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}} in places with “shima” (island) in their names, they represented the deification of the Agatsuma River itself.{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Studies on the Jinmyōchō of Kōzuke Province |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |pages=210-212 |last=Ozaki |author=Kizao Ozaki |first=Kizao |author-link=Kizao Ozaki |doi=10.11501/12266236}}{{Registration required}} An engraved inscription dated the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|lt=1711|Q6866|WD=Q6866|1=1711}}) is found on the {{ill|lt=gibōshi|Q3105399|WD=Q3105399|1=Giboshi}} of the balustrade of the main hall, and thus that year is considered the year of construction{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The shrine buildings are located atop a high ground known as Ubazuka{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. Although it was once thought to be a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, it is in fact a natural {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|p=117}}. There is a local tradition that this was the place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}’s wife {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwagitsuhime|1=UNKNOWN}} and their child lived, and that Ubazuka is the grave of Uwagitsuhime, which is said to be the origin of the place name Obashima{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1984|p=903}}. ===Grounds=== Kawasukune-jinja first torii.jpg|First torii Kawasukune-jinja first torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the first torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii.jpg|Second torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the second torii Kawasukune-jinja Worship Hall.jpg|Shrine buildings ===Festivals=== * April 3 – Spring annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * July 25 – Gion Festival; a festival of the enshrined Yasaka Shrine, during which a portable shrine is paraded{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}}. * October 9 – Autumn annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===Access=== * 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obashima Station|Q7876424|WD=Q7876424|1=Ubashima Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma Line|Q391568|WD=Q391568|1=Agatsuma Line}} of {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === {{Commonscat}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Gazetteer |date=1925-10-20 |year=1925 |publisher=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |ref=harv |author=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |doi=10.11501/1020918}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1993-3-31 |year=1993 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 2 General History, Part 1: Prehistory to Early Modern |doi=10.11501/9644681}}{{Registration required}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 4 Folklore |doi=10.11501/9643284}}{{Registration required}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:渋川市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Shibukawa, Gunma]] [[Category:Takeshiuchi no Sukune]]

{{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '群馬県渋川市祖母島499', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '群馬県吾妻郡東吾妻町箱島', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takeuchi-jinja}} [[JA:武内神社 (渋川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q66085129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takeuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Kawasukune-jinja approach.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}} Obashima 499 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), disputed shrine
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}) September 21 |Chief priest = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Sukune Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}}
{{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Takeuchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeuchi Shrine''' (たけうちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Obashima, {{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}}. The enshrined deity is Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma District|Q5617131|WD=Q5617131|1=Gunma District, Gunma}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===History=== It is said that the shrine originated from being divided from {{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Shibukawa City Kawashima)|Q66085130|WD=Q66085130|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Shibukawa)}} in Kawashima on September 21 of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}){{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. As indicated by a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} (1708) that records the name “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin,” the shrine was known as “Sukune Shrine” until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}}), it was renamed “Takeuchi Shrine”{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The {{ill|lt=tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} on the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears the inscription “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin.” {{ill|lt=Kizao Ozaki|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} proposed that Takeuchi Shrine (Sukune Shrine) was originally Kawanami no Sukune Shrine. He interpreted “Kawanami” as “river” and “Sukune” as “chokune,” understanding the shrine name to mean “the main current of the river.” He explained that because three shrines—{{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma Town)|Q66085131|WD=Q66085131|lt=Hakoshima|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma)}}, Obashima, and Kawashima—were lined up along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}} in places with “shima” (island) in their names, they represented the deification of the Agatsuma River itself.{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Studies on the Jinmyōchō of Kōzuke Province |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |pages=210-212 |last=Ozaki |author=Kizao Ozaki |first=Kizao |author-link=Kizao Ozaki |doi=10.11501/12266236}}{{Registration required}} An engraved inscription dated the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|lt=1711|Q6866|WD=Q6866|1=1711}}) is found on the {{ill|lt=gibōshi|Q3105399|WD=Q3105399|1=Giboshi}} of the balustrade of the main hall, and thus that year is considered the year of construction{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The shrine buildings are located atop a high ground known as Ubazuka{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. Although it was once thought to be a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, it is in fact a natural {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|p=117}}. There is a local tradition that this was the place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}’s wife {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwagitsuhime|1=UNKNOWN}} and their child lived, and that Ubazuka is the grave of Uwagitsuhime, which is said to be the origin of the place name Obashima{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1984|p=903}}. ===Grounds=== Kawasukune-jinja first torii.jpg|First torii Kawasukune-jinja first torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the first torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii.jpg|Second torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the second torii Kawasukune-jinja Worship Hall.jpg|Shrine buildings ===Festivals=== * April 3 – Spring annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * July 25 – Gion Festival; a festival of the enshrined Yasaka Shrine, during which a portable shrine is paraded{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}}. * October 9 – Autumn annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===Access=== * 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obashima Station|Q7876424|WD=Q7876424|1=Ubashima Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma Line|Q391568|WD=Q391568|1=Agatsuma Line}} of {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === {{Commonscat}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Gazetteer |date=1925-10-20 |year=1925 |publisher=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |ref=harv |author=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |doi=10.11501/1020918}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1993-3-31 |year=1993 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 2 General History, Part 1: Prehistory to Early Modern |doi=10.11501/9644681}}{{Registration required}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 4 Folklore |doi=10.11501/9643284}}{{Registration required}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:渋川市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Shibukawa, Gunma]] [[Category:Takeshiuchi no Sukune]]

{{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '群馬県渋川市祖母島499', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '群馬県吾妻郡東吾妻町箱島', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Takeuchi-jinja}} [[JA:武内神社 (渋川市)]] {{wikidata link|Q66085129}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Takeuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Kawasukune-jinja approach.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}} Obashima 499 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), disputed shrine
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}) September 21 |Chief priest = |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = Sukune Daimyōjin |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=April 3|Q2509|WD=Q2509|1=April 3}}
{{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Takeuchi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Takeuchi Shrine''' (たけうちじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in Obashima, {{ill|lt=Shibukawa City|Q851492|WD=Q851492|1=Shibukawa, Gunma}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}}. The enshrined deity is Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN|lt=minor Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Kōzuke Province|Q1054838|WD=Q1054838|1=Kōzuke Province}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma District|Q5617131|WD=Q5617131|1=Gunma District, Gunma}}. Its former shrine rank was {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|lt=Takeuchi Sukune-no-Mikoto|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * {{ill|lt=Ukemochi|Q2280323|WD=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}}{{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===History=== It is said that the shrine originated from being divided from {{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Shibukawa City Kawashima)|Q66085130|WD=Q66085130|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Shibukawa)}} in Kawashima on September 21 of the 1st year of {{ill|lt=Bunan|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} ({{ill|lt=1444|Q6420|WD=Q6420|1=1444}}){{Sfn|Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association|1925|p=587}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. As indicated by a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sōgen senji|1=UNKNOWN}} from the 5th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} (1708) that records the name “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin,” the shrine was known as “Sukune Shrine” until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. In the 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}}), it was renamed “Takeuchi Shrine”{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The {{ill|lt=tablet|Q5358873|WD=Q5358873|1=biane}} on the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} bears the inscription “Shōichi-i Sukune Daimyōjin.” {{ill|lt=Kizao Ozaki|Q21698487|WD=Q21698487|1=Kisao Ozaki}} proposed that Takeuchi Shrine (Sukune Shrine) was originally Kawanami no Sukune Shrine. He interpreted “Kawanami” as “river” and “Sukune” as “chokune,” understanding the shrine name to mean “the main current of the river.” He explained that because three shrines—{{ill|lt=Kawanami no Sukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma Town)|Q66085131|WD=Q66085131|lt=Hakoshima|1=Kawasukune Shrine (Higashiagatsuma)}}, Obashima, and Kawashima—were lined up along the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma River|Q1636255|WD=Q1636255|1=Agatsuma River}} in places with “shima” (island) in their names, they represented the deification of the Agatsuma River itself.{{Cite book|Japanese |title=Studies on the Jinmyōchō of Kōzuke Province |date=1974-12-10 |year=1974 |publisher=Ozaki Sensei Chosho Kankōkai |pages=210-212 |last=Ozaki |author=Kizao Ozaki |first=Kizao |author-link=Kizao Ozaki |doi=10.11501/12266236}}{{Registration required}} An engraved inscription dated the 8th year of {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|WD=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} ({{ill|lt=1711|Q6866|WD=Q6866|1=1711}}) is found on the {{ill|lt=gibōshi|Q3105399|WD=Q3105399|1=Giboshi}} of the balustrade of the main hall, and thus that year is considered the year of construction{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. The shrine buildings are located atop a high ground known as Ubazuka{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|pp=883-884}}. Although it was once thought to be a {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}, it is in fact a natural {{ill|lt=hill|Q222912|WD=Q222912|1=Hügelland}}{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1993|p=117}}. There is a local tradition that this was the place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}’s wife {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwagitsuhime|1=UNKNOWN}} and their child lived, and that Ubazuka is the grave of Uwagitsuhime, which is said to be the origin of the place name Obashima{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1984|p=903}}. ===Grounds=== Kawasukune-jinja first torii.jpg|First torii Kawasukune-jinja first torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the first torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii.jpg|Second torii Kawasukune-jinja second torii tablet.jpg|Tablet of the second torii Kawasukune-jinja Worship Hall.jpg|Shrine buildings ===Festivals=== * April 3 – Spring annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} * July 25 – Gion Festival; a festival of the enshrined Yasaka Shrine, during which a portable shrine is paraded{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}}. * October 9 – Autumn annual festival{{Sfn|Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee|1986|p=542}} ===Access=== * 10-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Obashima Station|Q7876424|WD=Q7876424|1=Ubashima Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Agatsuma Line|Q391568|WD=Q391568|1=Agatsuma Line}} of {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === {{Commonscat}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Gazetteer |date=1925-10-20 |year=1925 |publisher=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |ref=harv |author=Gunma Prefecture Gunma District Education Association |doi=10.11501/1020918}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1993-3-31 |year=1993 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 2 General History, Part 1: Prehistory to Early Modern |doi=10.11501/9644681}}{{Registration required}} * {{Cite book|Japanese |title=History of Shibukawa City |date=1984-10-1 |year=1984 |publisher=Shibukawa City |ref=harv |editor=Shibukawa City History Compilation Committee |volume=Vol. 4 Folklore |doi=10.11501/9643284}}{{Registration required}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Takeuchi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:渋川市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Shibukawa, Gunma]] [[Category:Takeshiuchi no Sukune]]
